findings.problem: The repaired Makefile adds the required check target, but the standalone valgrind-test target still only depends on build/sysdiff and reuses whatever binary is present. make-quality avoids the known ASan/UBSan conflict by cleaning and rebuilding with GCC before invoking valgrind-test, but direct make valgrind-test remains unreliable immediately after make sanitizer-test leaves a sanitizer-instrumented binary in build/.
findings.proposed_fix: Make valgrind-test force a clean non-sanitized build before running Valgrind, or encode a clear Makefile-level precondition so standalone use after sanitizer-test is not mistaken for a robust quality gate.

checks_run.summary: Confirmed the required make-quality target text is present in the Makefile.
Dry-run of the aggregate quality target completed successfully.

target-contract.justification: The Makefile exposes make-quality, check, and clean. check delegates to test-suite, preserving the existing shell and pytest coverage path.

dry-run-compatibility.justification: make -n make-quality exits 0 and expands the intended aggregate sequence, satisfying the immediate dry-run repair requirement.

quality-gate-robustness.justification: make-quality rebuilds before Valgrind, but the standalone valgrind-test target still reuses build/sysdiff and remains vulnerable to the previously recorded sanitizer-binary conflict.
